title: podman images from one server to another not working
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

I have 2 RHEL 8.8 servers, running podman 4.4.1, one has internet (development) and the other (production) does not.

The development server works fine as podman is able to pull the images directly using internet, but then I create and move the image with "podman save" and "podman load" command (I have also tried "podman import") to the production environment and it does not work.

I have tried this a few times but always found the same outcome.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. On the development server, create a tarball as below

$ podman save -o cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar docker.io/confluentinc/cp-zookeeper:7.1.1
Copying blob f2c4302f03b8 done
Copying blob 60609ec85f86 done
Copying blob 5693d08c15ed done
Copying blob 37cafaf80b7e done
Copying blob 936ea683b512 done
Copying blob d117415e61f8 done
Copying blob f67583d3da0a done
Copying blob c8c571a6a27d done
Copying blob a93c87792774 done
Copying blob 3bfddb9a813d done
Copying blob c898263a4c9c done
Copying config 534c2e2cfd done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

3. On the production, tried to impor as well as load but no joy

$ podman load -i cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d117415e61f8 done
Copying blob 60609ec85f86 done
Copying blob f2c4302f03b8 done
Copying blob 5693d08c15ed done
Copying blob 936ea683b512 done
Copying blob 37cafaf80b7e done
Copying blob f67583d3da0a done
Copying blob c8c571a6a27d done
Copying blob a93c87792774 done
Copying blob c898263a4c9c done
Copying blob 3bfddb9a813d done
Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats:
 * oci: initializing source oci:cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar:: open cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar/index.json: not a directory
 * oci-archive: loading index: open /var/tmp/oci1034655130/index.json: no such file or directory
 * docker-archive: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:f2c4302f03b8582b388fb66058da2ae3a3b70cff3ab8ce3ebfc0cc24349814b9": processing tar file(potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 0:12 for /var/spool/mail): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run podman-system-migrate: lchown /var/spool/mail: invalid argument): exit status 1
 * dir: open cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar/manifest.json: not a directory
$
$
$
$ podman import cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar cp/zookeeper:7.1.1
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 16c4955e07b2 skipped: already exists
Copying config 98f7a9e904 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
sha256:98f7a9e90417aaddc5c1b61d0d12e075bbf9e2b092c00cc900c8c789d419eed6
$
$ podman run -d -it cp/zookeeper:7.1.1
Error: no command or entrypoint provided, and no CMD or ENTRYPOINT from image
$


5.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

On the production server, I get the following


$ podman load -i cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d117415e61f8 done
Copying blob 60609ec85f86 done
Copying blob f2c4302f03b8 done
Copying blob 5693d08c15ed done
Copying blob 936ea683b512 done
Copying blob 37cafaf80b7e done
Copying blob f67583d3da0a done
Copying blob c8c571a6a27d done
Copying blob a93c87792774 done
Copying blob c898263a4c9c done
Copying blob 3bfddb9a813d done
Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats:
 * oci: initializing source oci:cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar:: open cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar/index.json: not a directory
 * oci-archive: loading index: open /var/tmp/oci1034655130/index.json: no such file or directory
 * docker-archive: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:f2c4302f03b8582b388fb66058da2ae3a3b70cff3ab8ce3ebfc0cc24349814b9": processing tar file(potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 0:12 for /var/spool/mail): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run podman-system-migrate: lchown /var/spool/mail: invalid argument): exit status 1
 * dir: open cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar/manifest.json: not a directory
$
$
$
$ podman import cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar cp/zookeeper:7.1.1
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 16c4955e07b2 skipped: already exists
Copying config 98f7a9e904 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
sha256:98f7a9e90417aaddc5c1b61d0d12e075bbf9e2b092c00cc900c8c789d419eed6
$
$ podman run -d -it cp/zookeeper:7.1.1
Error: no command or entrypoint provided, and no CMD or ENTRYPOINT from image
$



### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

I expect it to work as it does in development

$ podman load -i cp-zookeeper.7.1.1.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d117415e61f8 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 5693d08c15ed skipped: already exists
Copying blob 37cafaf80b7e skipped: already exists
Copying blob 936ea683b512 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 60609ec85f86 skipped: already exists
Copying blob f67583d3da0a skipped: already exists
Copying blob f2c4302f03b8 skipped: already exists
Copying blob a93c87792774 skipped: already exists
Copying blob c8c571a6a27d skipped: already exists
Copying blob 3bfddb9a813d skipped: already exists
Copying blob c898263a4c9c skipped: already exists
Copying config 534c2e2cfd done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Loaded image: docker.io/confluentinc/cp-zookeeper:7.1.1
$
$ podman images | grep zoo
docker.io/confluentinc/cp-zookeeper  7.1.1       534c2e2cfdbc  20 months ago  797 MB


### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details
both RHELs are virtual machines

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
you do not have enough IDs available in the rootless user namespace.

You need to allocate more IDs to your user.

Please take a look at the subuid(5) man page for more information on how to do it.

> you do not have enough IDs available in the rootless user namespace.
> 
> You need to allocate more IDs to your user.
> 
> Please take a look at the subuid(5) man page for more information on how to do it.

Thanks, I thought that is required when you actually run the container and here I  struggling to even load the image.

How is this not a problem when pulled from a public repository but doesn't work when the same image is archived and loaded on another machine?
